## Exercise sheet

Student-facing compiled sheet assembled from reusable exercise objects.

- Exercises: 2
- Estimated time: 20 minutes
- Linked concepts: Instrumental Variables Intuition
- Course suitability: EC202 - Applied Econometrics

## Exercise 1: IV intuition check

- Type: conceptual
- Difficulty: easy
- Estimated time: 8 minutes

## Quick check

Suppose distance to a training center changes program participation. Explain which fact would support relevance and which fact would threaten exclusion.

## Exercise 2: IV assumption sort

- Type: conceptual
- Difficulty: medium
- Estimated time: 12 minutes

## Assumption sort

Label each statement as relevance, exclusion, or not enough information.

1. Commuting distance strongly predicts who takes the training program.
2. Places farther from the training center also have fewer employers hiring this month.
